title: Mailchimp vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and GetResponse email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool wins for your needs.
h1: Mailchimp vs GetResponse: Complete Comparison Guide
intro: Both Mailchimp and GetResponse are established email marketing platforms offering freemium models, but they cater to different user needs. Mailchimp excels as an all-in-one marketing suite for beginners and small businesses, while GetResponse is purpose-built for online entrepreneurs, course creators, and webinar hosts. Understanding their core strengths will help you choose the right platform for your specific use case.
choose_a_if: You're a beginner looking for an intuitive, easy-to-use email marketing platform
You need affordable pricing and want to start with basic email campaigns
You're running an ecommerce business and need strong landing page and automation features
choose_b_if: You host webinars or create online courses and need these features integrated
You want a comprehensive funnel-building platform for digital product sales
You're willing to pay more for specialized tools beyond traditional email marketing

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your needs. Mailchimp is better for beginners and traditional email marketing, while GetResponse excels for online course creators and webinar hosts. Mailchimp offers lower pricing and superior user-friendliness, whereas GetResponse provides more specialized features for digital entrepreneurs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or GetResponse?
faq.a: Mailchimp is cheaper, starting at $13/month compared to GetResponse's $19/month. Both offer free tiers supporting 500 subscribers. However, the best value depends on whether you need GetResponse's webinar and course features—which could justify the higher cost if those tools replace separate subscriptions.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, switching is possible. Both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and email templates. You'll need to manually recreate automations and campaigns in Mailchimp, as the platforms have different automation builders. Plan for some setup time during the migration process.
